entities.name: VIABILITY
entities.description: The ability of a seed to germinate and grow into a plant

entities.name: SEED QUALITY
entities.description: The overall quality and health of the seed

entities.name: SEED
entities.description: The biological seed used in breeding and research

entities.name: STORAGE FACILITIES
entities.description: Facilities designed for the safe storage of seeds

entities.name: DRYING
entities.description: The process of reducing moisture content in seeds or plant samples

entities.name: MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of water present in seeds or plant samples

entities.name: BREEDERS' SEED
entities.description: High-quality seed produced by breeders for further multiplication

entities.name: EXPERIMENT STATION
entities.description: A facility where agricultural research and seed testing are conducted

entities.name: YIELD TRIALS
entities.description: Experiments conducted to evaluate the yield of different crop varieties

entities.name: COMPARATIVE ANALYSIS
entities.description: The process of comparing different samples to draw conclusions

entities.name: PLANT SAMPLES
entities.description: Biological samples of plants collected for research analysis

relationships.description: Drying is used to reduce moisture content
Moisture content levels affect the viability of seeds
Moisture content levels influence overall seed quality
Drying is a necessary process for seeds before storage
Storage facilities are used to maintain seeds
Experiment stations manage and store germplasm
Experiment stations manage and store breeders' seed
Yield trials provide the material and data for comparative analysis
Plant samples are dried for subsequent analysis
Proper moisture content is required for safe storage in facilities
